chapter_title.ar: باب مَوْلِدِ النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ وَفَاتِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 111 – Nazool (Arrival) of the Prophet<sup>saww</sup> and his<sup>saww</sup> demise

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/208)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/208)
text: إِنَّ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) قَالَ إِنَّ اللَّهَ مَثَّلَ لِي أُمَّتِي فِي الطِّينِ وَ عَلَّمَنِي أَسْمَاءَهُمْ كَمَا عَلَّمَ آدَمَ الْأَسْمَاءَ كُلَّهَا فَمَرَّ بِي أَصْحَابُ الرَّايَاتِ فَاسْتَغْفَرْتُ لِعَلِيٍّ وَ شِيعَتِهِ إِنَّ رَبِّي وَعَدَنِي فِي شِيعَةِ عَلِيٍّ خَصْلَةً قِيلَ يَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ وَ مَا هِيَ قَالَ الْمَغْفِرَةُ لِمَنْ آمَنَ مِنْهُمْ وَ أَنْ لَا يُغَادِرَ مِنْهُمْ صَغِيرَةً وَ لَا كَبِيرَةً وَ لَهُمْ تُبَدَّلُ السَّيِّئَاتُ حَسَنَاتٍ .

en.hubeali: A nmber of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ibn Fazzal, from Abu Jameela, from Muhammad Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Made a resemblance of my<sup>saww</sup> community for me<sup>saww</sup> in the clay and Taught me<sup>saww</sup> their names just as He<sup>azwj</sup> had Taught Adam<sup>saww</sup> the names, all of them. So the owners of the flags passed by me<sup>saww</sup>, and they sought Forgiveness for Ali<sup>asws</sup> and his<sup>asws</sup> Shias. My<sup>saww</sup> Lord<sup>azwj</sup> Promised me<sup>saww</sup> a characteristic with regards to the Shias of Ali<sup>asws</sup>’. It was said, ‘O Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>! And what is it?’ He<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘The Forgiveness for the ones from them who believe and that neither a minor (sin) nor a major (sin) of theirs would be left, and the evil deeds would be changed to good deeds’.
en.sarwar: 14. A number of our people has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn Sayf from ‘Amr ibn Shimr from Jabir who has said the following. “I asked abu Ja‘far (a.s.), ‘describe to me the Prophet (s.a) of Allah.” The Imam (a.s.) said, “The Holy Prophet (s.a) has a white reddish complexion, large balck eyes, the eyebrows almost joined, chubby hands and feet as if gold molded as to the form of his fingers and toes and with manifest shoulder bones. When he would turn to a person he would so with the whole of his body. Due to kind and tender heartiness. A line of hair extended from the front cavity of his neck down to his bellybutton as if it was a clear and pure silver lining. His neck was tall and clear, his nose would almost touch the water when he wanted to drink and when walking he would somehow lean forwards as if walking on a downward slope. No one similar to him was seen before or after the Holy Prophet (s.a) of Allah.”
